methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
FlyBy Robotics is a Seed-stage defense and security robotics company founded by Jason H. Lu and Cat Orman, having raised $5M+ to date. The valuation universe comprises 10 private and 2 public comparables in the Defense & Security sector, including later-stage peers such as Anduril Industries, Helsing, and Shield AI, as well as public references DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable and yields the primary valuation signal. Using total capital raised of $5M as a proxy for seed-stage valuation, a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier to next stage implies a forward valuation of $12M. This reflects conventional Seed-to-Series A venture progression in the sector. A peer-median approach could not be applied; while 19 same-sector comparables exist, insufficient narrow-set peers meeting stage and tier criteria precluded reliable triangulation via this method.

Analysis supports a valuation range of $11M to $14M, centered at $12M. This range reflects the single applicable method and inherent seed-stage uncertainty. Key risks to valuation include market timing for Series A fundraising, competitive positioning relative to the named later-stage peers, and actual near-term revenue generation. The wide comp universe in Defense & Security provides sector context but limited direct validation at this early stage.
